ucs.title: [LLM] Saint Bear (UAC-0056) Saint Bot / OutSteel delivery — Office or browser spawns script interpreter that drops PE to %AppData%/%Temp%
ucs.description: Detects the canonical Saint Bear (Lorec53 / TA471 / Storm-0587) initial-access-to-install chain: a Ukrainian-government-themed phishing attachment or web-staged ZIP/SFX is opened, Office/Explorer/browser spawns wscript/cscript/mshta/powershell, which in turn writes a Saint Bot loader or OutSteel stealer binary into %AppData%\Roaming, %AppData%\Local\Temp, or %Users%\Public within seconds. This is the exact tradecraft Unit 42, CERT-UA, and Cybereason document for UAC-0056 since 2021.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action=created AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.exe" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.scr" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.cpl") AND Filesystem.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.process_guid Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.user | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | join type=inner dest process_guid [ | tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.process_guid Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` ] | where firstSeen >= relative_time(now(),"-7d") | table firstSeen dest user parent_process_name process_name process file_name file_path
ucs.kql:
let win = 180s;
let ScriptFromUser = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| project ScriptTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
          ScriptParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ScriptFile  = FileName,
          ScriptCmd   = ProcessCommandLine;
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")  // Saint Bot / OutSteel drop sites
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".dll"
     or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".cpl"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
| join kind=inner ScriptFromUser on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (ScriptTime .. ScriptTime + win)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          DroppedFile = FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, FileOriginUrl,
          ScriptParent, ScriptFile, ScriptCmd,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, ScriptTime)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let win = 180s;
let ScriptFromUser = ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where ParentProcessName has_any ("winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe","outlook.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","explorer.exe","7zfm.exe","winrar.exe")
| where TargetProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| project ScriptTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ScriptParent = ParentProcessName,
          ScriptFile   = TargetProcessName,
          ScriptCmd    = TargetProcessCommandLine;
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Users\Public\")
| where TargetFileName endswith ".exe" or TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
     or TargetFileName endswith ".scr" or TargetFileName endswith ".cpl"
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe")
| join kind=inner ScriptFromUser on DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (ScriptTime .. ScriptTime + win)
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DroppedFile = TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256,
          ScriptParent, ScriptFile, ScriptCmd,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, ScriptTime)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Saint Bear's defining initial-access pattern (Unit 42 'OutSteel / Saint Bot' report, CERT-UA UAC-0056 alerts) is a Ukrainian-government-spoofed phish carrying a script (.js/.vbs/.lnk/.hta) inside an SFX/ZIP that, when executed, uses wscript/cscript/mshta/powershell to fetch and stage the Saint Bot loader or OutSteel stealer in %AppData% or %Temp%. Joining Office/Explorer/browser → script-interpret

ucs.title: [LLM] Saint Bot Run-key persistence — script interpreter writes %AppData% binary path to HKCU\...\Run
ucs.description: Hunts Saint Bear's Saint Bot persistence step: after the dropper unpacks, the same script interpreter (wscript/cscript/cmd/powershell/rundll32) immediately registers an autorun under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run (or RunOnce) whose value points back into %AppData%\Roaming, %AppData%\Local\Temp, or %Users%\Public. This combination is rare among legitimate installers (which use MSI/Setup) and is documented across Saint Bot samples since 2021.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.action=modified AND (Registry.registry_path="*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce*") AND (Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Registry.registry_value_data="*\\Users\\Public\\*") AND Registry.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by host Registry.dest Registry.user Registry.process_name Registry.process Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_name Registry.registry_value_data | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)` | where NOT match(user, "(?i).*\\$$") | table firstSeen dest user process_name process registry_path registry_value_name registry_value_data
ucs.kql:
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run",
                            @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce",
                            @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (@"AppData\Roaming", @"AppData\Local\Temp", @"\Users\Public\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe",
                                       "powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe",
                                       "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData,
          ScriptInterpreter = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ScriptCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          GrandParent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
ImRegistryEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where EventType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run",
                            @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce",
                            @"\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run")
| where RegistryValueData has_any (@"AppData\Roaming", @"AppData\Local\Temp", @"\Users\Public\")
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe",
                                   "powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe",
                                   "rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe")
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData,
          ScriptInterpreter = ActingProcessName,
          ScriptCmd = ActingProcessCommandLine
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Saint Bear / Saint Bot Run-Key Persistence via Script Interpreter
id: 7c9d2f4a-1e3b-4b7d-9a6f-saintbear0002
status: experimental
description: Detects Saint Bear (UAC-0056 / Lorec53 / TA471) Saint Bot persistence — a script interpreter writes an HKCU Run/RunOnce value pointing at a binary in %AppData% or %Users%\Public.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1031/
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/ukraine-targeted-outsteel-saintbot/
author: SOC detection engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1547.001
  - attack.t1112
  - attack.g1031
logsource:
  product: windows
  category: registry_set
detection:
  selection_key:
    TargetObject|contains:
      - '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run'
      - '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\RunOnce'
      - '\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer\Run'
  selection_value:
    Details|contains:
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
  filter_machine:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: all of selection_* and not filter_machine
falsepositives:
  - Portable applications that legitimately install themselves to %AppData% (rare; review parent chain)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Saint Bot's persistence routine — well documented in Unit 42, Malwarebytes, and CERT-UA UAC-0056 reports — sets HKCU Run with a value pointing into %AppData% from a non-MSI, non-Setup parent (typically the same wscript/cscript that just dropped the binary in UC1). Legitimate software almost always writes Run keys via msiexec.exe, setup*.exe, or Explorer; a script interpreter doing it from a user c
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
